verdict_reason: The traditional reading is: the avipaścit speakers utter visually beautiful but substance-thin speech, are devoted specifically to the kāmya-karma portion of the Vedas, and declare 'na anyad asti' as a consequence of their attachment-driven partial reading. The engineering layer maps this to: a class of organisational speech (polished standing-review metrics) which is devoted to one bounded portion of the available signal (conversion-rate, feature-count) and whose pathology is the implicit closure clause. The reverse-translation: 'an engineering team devoted to one bounded portion of the available signal as if it were the whole, with an implicit there-is-nothing-else closure' translates back to veda-vāda-rata avipaścit declaring 'na anyad asti'. No distortion. The Sanskrit's specifically scoped critique (kāmya-portion-fixation, not Veda-rejection) is preserved as 'exclusive fixation, not anti-metric'. PASS for both 3.6 and 7.10.
